parsed_review.summary: CP00-595 is a descriptor-only closeout pack for RP19 client-portal-core (P05 closeout / P06 foundation, 150 units, risk C, range RP19.P05.M06.S06-RP19.P06.M03.S14). The pack-level claim is correctly stated as production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, status=implemented_pending_review; the production_ready_flag is a verification label, not an enterprise-trust claim. The claim is fully supported: registry binding, contract current_pack + p05_closeout_p06_foundation_descriptor projection, live closeout-pack-plan.json, and manifest all agree on identity, counts, and distribution (fixture29/claude_review8/implementation59/security_audit22/test16/hermes_evidence4/ui12 = 150). All 7 required CP595 public exports exist (service.js + validators.js) and are surfaced via index.js export *. Descriptor output is descriptor-only with comprehensive no-write/no-real-data flags (freezeCp595Result), and every section row carries descriptor_only=true/runtime_execution=false/customer_safe_errors_only=true/raw_payload_included=false. Validators (coverage, slice, hermes, claude, handoff) and three dedicated tests assert these boundaries plus the authority boundary; the contract validator script deep-equals the contract projection, requirements, and no-write attestation. Authority boundaries hold: Claude is a read-only reviewer (Read/Grep/Glob), not final approver; promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false; human approval required for runtime opening; enterprise trust not claimed from local validation. Hermes patterns are descriptor-only review packet/receipt handling with emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false and no product-runtime embedding. Review-hardening preconditions are satisfied: forbidden_review_evidence enumerates all CP178+ failure modes, and the readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, non-zero stdout, valid JSON), so no auth-failure/0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped evidence is being relied upon. command-evidence records 28 commands at exit 0 (npm test, npm run validate, all RP validators, plan/weighted/spec/control-plane/goal/fullplan, git diff --check). Two modified progress-control-room UI files are correctly classified as unrelated/out-of-scope. One non-blocking P3: P06 security_audit/permission/ui rows are bare descriptor placeholders (no enforcement/audit/trimming logic), which is by-design for a descriptor foundation and consistent with production_ready=false. No P0/P1/P2 issues; pack closeout and goal closeout are not blocked. Final human approval remains required for any enterprise-trust/runtime claim.

findings.title: P06 security/permission/ui rows resolve to bare descriptor placeholders, not runtime enforcement
findings.details: CP595_ROW_EXTRAS only spreads CP594_ROW_EXTRAS, and there are no ROW_EXTRAS keyed to the P06 permission/security row titles (permission_matrix_row, view/search/mutation/export/share/ai_retrieval decision bindings, deny_over_allow_check, matched_rule_capture, audit_hint_fields, security_trimming_proof, audit_event_expectation, permission_fixture, legal_hold/ethical_wall/object_acl interaction). These rows (covering the pack's 22 security_audit, 12 ui, and permission-binding implementation units) therefore resolve to descriptor rows carrying only the four base flags (descriptor_only, runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only, raw_payload_included:false). No permission decision logic, audit emission, or security trimming is implemented in this pack. This is by design for a descriptor-only foundation and is internally consistent (validators and tests accept bare descriptor rows; identical pattern used in CP587-CP594), and it reinforces rather than contradicts production_ready=false / runtime_ready=false. Noted so the human final approver does not mistake the security_audit/ui deliverable counts for implemented runtime controls.
findings.recommendation: No change required for this descriptor pack. Ensure the downstream runtime-opening packs (CP00-596+), gated by human approval, implement and test the actual permission enforcement, deny-over-allow, audit emission, and security-trimming behavior that these P06 rows currently only describe before any enterprise-trust or production_ready=true claim is made.
